description: A dual-axis analysis framework for systematically researching products, companies, concepts, or people. The vertical axis traces the complete lifecycle, the horizontal axis expands into current competitive comparison.

subject.name: string — Name of the research subject
subject.type: enum: product | company | concept | person | other
subject.one_line_definition: string — Define what this thing is in one sentence
subject.researcher: string — Who is conducting this research
subject.research_motivation: string — Why research this, what was the trigger

vertical_axis_lifecycle.description: Complete timeline from origin to present, presented as narrative — the more detailed and multifaceted, the better

origin.birth_context: string — The era, industry state, and technological environment when it was born
origin.birth_motivation: string — Why did it appear? What problem did it solve? What gap did it fill?

founders_or_initiators.background: string — What had these people done before, why were they the ones to do this
founders_or_initiators.initial_vision: string — What they originally wanted to build

origin.early_form: string — What it looked like initially, how it differs from today
origin.seed_event: string — Was there a key event or inspiration that directly prompted its creation

key_milestone_timeline.why_important: string — How this event changed the subsequent trajectory
key_milestone_timeline.decisions_and_choices: string — What choices were made at this node, what was abandoned
key_milestone_timeline.external_trigger: string | null — Was it triggered by external events (policy, competitor moves, market changes)
key_milestone_timeline.results_and_impact: string — Direct and indirect consequences of this event

phase_segmentation.phase_name: string — e.g.: inception, rapid growth, transformation, maturity
phase_segmentation.core_characteristics: string — The most prominent features of this phase
phase_segmentation.core_tensions: string — The biggest challenge or internal tension during this phase
key_figures: string — People who played key roles during this phase
key_decisions: string — Major decisions made during this phase
phase_segmentation.phase_outcomes: string — What results were delivered by the end of this phase

lock_in_decisions: string — Which early decisions locked in later development direction, becoming irreversible
missed_forks: string — Which key moments could have gone a different way
path_dependency_analysis.self_reinforcing_mechanisms: string — What mechanisms deepened the path (network effects, ecosystem lock-in, user habits, etc.)

narrative_threads.main_story: string — If the entire journey could be summarized in one story, what would it be
recurring_themes: string — Patterns, tensions, or themes that appear repeatedly across different phases
unresolved_mysteries: string — What information is still missing and needs further investigation

horizontal_axis_competitive_comparison.description: At the current point in time, systematic horizontal comparison with competitors/peers
horizontal_axis_competitive_comparison.analysis_time_snapshot: date — The baseline point in time for horizontal comparison

subject_profile.current_positioning: string — How it currently defines itself
subject_profile.business_model: string — How it makes money / sustains operations
subject_profile.user_scale_and_growth: string — Current scale and growth trend

typical_use_cases: string — Main scenarios where users use it

competitor_list.key_difference_from_subject: string — Compared to the research subject, what is the most fundamental difference
competitor_list.threat_analysis: string — Why it constitutes this level of threat

dimensional_comparison_matrix.description: Select the most critical dimensions and compare the subject against all competitors

comparison_dimensions.dimension_name: string — e.g.: performance, pricing, usability, ecosystem, community activity

competitive_landscape_assessment.current_landscape: string — Is it a hundred flowers blooming, a duopoly, a monopoly, or something else
competitive_landscape_assessment.landscape_formation_reasons: string — Why is it this landscape
competitive_landscape_assessment.landscape_evolution_trend: string — Where might it head next
competitive_landscape_assessment.subject_position: string — Where does the subject sit in this landscape

cross_axis_insights.description: Cross-analysis of vertical and horizontal axes — the most valuable part of HV Analysis
cross_axis_insights.how_history_shaped_current_position: string — Which decisions and events from the vertical trajectory determined its position in today's horizontal comparison
cross_axis_insights.longitudinal_comparison_of_competitors: string — If competitors are also placed on the timeline, how do their origins and evolutions differ
historical_roots_of_current_advantages: string — Each current advantage traced back to which historical node
historical_roots_of_current_disadvantage: string — Each current weakness traced back to which historical decision

future_projection.based_on_vertical_trends: string — If the current trajectory continues, what happens next
future_projection.based_on_horizontal_competition: string — How will changes in the competitive landscape affect it

output_requirements.vertical_output: Presented as narrative/story, not a timeline list. Must have causal logic, details, people — let readers feel 'why it arrived where it is today step by step'
output_requirements.horizontal_output: Presented as comparative analysis, with both structured dimensional comparison and qualitative judgments and insights
output_requirements.cross_axis_output: Presented as analytical commentary, weaving information from both axes to produce original judgments
output_requirements.suggested_word_count: Vertical 3,000–8,000 words, Horizontal 2,000–5,000 words, Cross-axis 1,000–2,000 words
output_requirements.source_attribution: All key facts must cite information sources (links, documents, interviews, etc.)
